narrative: OMX30 opens into a mixed backdrop today. Global equities rolled over overnight—DAX, CAC, and Nikkei all sharply lower—creating a negative correlation signal (-1.128%) that typically precedes 0.2–0.5% opening weakness in Stockholm. While OMX30 technicals remain constructive (bull regime, +7% vs 200-MA, MACD positive), the weakness is structural rather than tactical: ADX is very weak at 5.1, which cuts conviction by ~30%, and VIX's sharp spike to 20.22 signals risk-off rotation. The one bright spot is USD/SEK strength (+0.92%), which should support large-cap exporters, but NASDAQ's -1.32% tumble undermines sentiment for tech and industrial bellwethers. Watch the open carefully for follow-through selling or a technical bounce off 3085 (lower Bollinger).
morningBrief: OMX30 set for a modest red open (-0.2% to -0.5%) as European indices crumble and Asia's risk-off bleeds through. DAX leading the decline at -1.45% creates immediate headwind; VIX surging to 20.22 signals volatility regime shift. However, technicals are not in full breakdown: we remain +7% above the 200-MA, MACD is above signal, and Bollinger %B sits at 0.56 (midrange). Key watch: support at 3085 (lower band) and yesterday's close at 3123—if we hold both on open, consider tactically long via Atlas Copco, Volvo, and Ericsson into any bounce. The USD/SEK strength (+0.92%) is a tailwind for large-cap exporters, but muted by broad risk appetite drain. Financials (banks) are mixed: yields down 27bp overnight pressure NIM relief hopes. Real estate (Balder, Castellum) vulnerable to refinancing cost reset. Watch Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET for catalyst; any surprise could trigger fresh selloff or stabilization.
drivers: Broad European weakness (DAX -1.45%, CAC -0.96%) flowing into OMX30
Asian contagion from Nikkei -3.55% overnight signals risk-off sentiment
VIX spike +17.01% to 20.22 indicates elevated volatility despite bull technicals
USD/SEK strength (+0.92%) supports exporters but offset by NASDAQ weakness (-1.32%)
Weak ADX (5.1) reduces technical conviction; RSI 47.7 neutral with mean-reversion risk

sectorImpacts.reason: European industrial weakness (DAX -1.45%) and NASDAQ sentiment spillover pressures demand outlook. USD/SEK strength partially offsets.
US 10Y yield down 27bp to 4.451% compresses NIM expansion thesis. Risk-off mode may trigger deposit flight.
NASDAQ -1.32% overnight creates negative sentiment contagion. Weak ADX provides reduced conviction.
Risk-off environment and VIX spike trigger defensive rotation. Relative outperformance likely vs cyclicals.
Copper -2.66% and gold -1.42% overnight are severe headwinds. Commodity weakness outweighs USD/SEK support.
Yields down but risk-off mode pressures valuations and refinancing confidence. Negative real rate environment unwind risk.
Oil down -0.40% is minor tailwind, but broad risk-off and VIX spike hurt discretionary sentiment. EUR/SEK up 0.43% pressures H&M sourcing costs.

watchList.reason: USD/SEK +0.92% is structural exporter support; if OMX30 bounces off 3085, Volvo export upside likely. Hedge industrial sector downturn.
USD/SEK tailwind offsets some DAX weakness. Watch for accumulation on any dip toward 380–390 SEK.
Defensive bid in risk-off environment. VIX spike may drive rotation into pharma. Monitor for buy-the-dip entry.
Copper -2.66% and gold -1.42% are severe. Wait for stabilization before re-engagement; downside risks clear.
Yields down pressure NIM. Risk-off sentiment mixed. Wait for Jobless Claims data at 14:30 CET before positioning.
NASDAQ -1.32% overnight creates negative spillover. Weak ADX limits conviction for technical bounce. Reduce exposure.

econCalendarAlert: US Jobless Claims at 14:30 CET—key volatility catalyst. No major Swedish data releases. Monitor ECB/Fed rhetoric for rate expectations reset.
topRisk: Further cascade selling if Jobless Claims disappoint or if Asian weakness spreads to European open. VIX >22 could trigger margin calls and liquidation across OMX30. Conversely, strong US jobs data could stabilize sentiment and unlock bounce.
technicalSummary: MACD above signal line with positive histogram +4.702 (momentum support)
RSI 47.7 is neutral but near mean-reversion zone; watch for dips below 40 or spikes above 60
ADX 5.1 (very weak)—reduce position sizing by ~30% and expect choppiness
Bollinger %B 0.56 mid-band; upper half intact but trending toward compression as width 4.67%
Stochastic K 65.4, D 86.8 shows neutral with D elevated—potential overbought pullback
52W position 89% of range; -2.7% from high leaves room for further downside if trend breaks
